docs.title: ISOSTER Code Review: Core Algorithm Issues and Performance Analysis
1. Eccentric Anomaly (EA) Mode Implementation Inconsistency
docs.text:
The EA mode implementation has a fundamental issue in the harmonic fitting approach:

Problem: In sampling.py:extract_isophote_data(), when EA mode is enabled: - It samples uniformly in ψ (eccentric anomaly) ✓ - Converts ψ → φ for coordinate calculation ✓ - BUT: Returns angles=psi for harmonic fitting, but phi=phi for geometry updates

Inconsistency: The comments suggest that for EA mode, harmonics should be fitted in ψ space, but the gradient computation in fitting.py:compute_gradient() always uses φ-based sampling, regardless of EA mode. This creates a mismatch between the fitting space and gradient computation space.

Impact: For high-ellipticity galaxies (ε > 0.3), this could lead to biased geometry updates because the gradient computation doesn't match the sampling space.

Fix: Either: 1. Always use φ-based sampling for consistency (slower but more consistent) 2. Implement ψ-based gradient computation for EA mode (more complex but faster)

docs.title: 2. Numerical Stability Issues
docs.text:
Edge Case in EA Conversion (sampling.py:eccentric_anomaly_to_position_angle): 

position_angle = np.arctan2(
    -(1 - ellipticity) * np.sin(eccentric_anomaly),
    np.cos(eccentric_anomaly)
)

Problem: For ellipticity near 1.0 (extremely flattened ellipses), the coefficient -(1 - ellipticity) becomes very small, leading to numerical instability.

Small SMA Regularization: The regularization penalty calculation: 

lambda_sma = config.central_reg_strength * np.exp(-(sma / config.central_reg_sma_threshold)**2)
For very small SMAs (< 0.5), this can underflow to zero or create division-by-zero issues later.

docs.title: 3. Logic Error in Gradient Computation
docs.text:
Problem in fitting.py:compute_gradient(): 

if gradient >= (previous_gradient / 3.0):
    # ... second derivative calculation
    if gradient >= (previous_gradient / 3.0):
        gradient = previous_gradient * 0.8  # Silent fallback

Issue: The gradient >= (previous_gradient / 3.0) condition is used twice, and the second usage doesn't update gradient_error, potentially leading to stale gradient error values.

docs.title: 1. Redundant Sampling Operations
docs.text:
In fitting.py:fit_isophote(), the most significant performance issue:

# Called once for harmonics fitting
data = extract_isophote_data(image, mask, x0, y0, sma, eps, pa, ...)
# Called again for gradient computation  
gradient, gradient_error = compute_gradient(..., current_data=(phi, intens), ...)

Problem: The same isophote data is extracted twice per iteration - once for harmonic fitting and once for gradient computation. For large images with many iterations, this is wasteful.

Fix: Pass the already-extracted data to gradient computation: 

# Extract once
data = extract_isophote_data(...)
# Use the same data for both fitting and gradient
angles, phi, intens, radii = data
# ... harmonic fitting ...
# Gradient computation with extracted data
gradient = compute_gradient_with_data(intens, previous_intens, sma, step)

docs.title: 2. Inefficient Aperture Photometry
docs.text:
Problem in fitting.py:compute_aperture_photometry(): 

y, x = np.mgrid[y_min:y_max, x_min:x_max]
# Creates full coordinate grids for every isophote

Issue: For large images with many isophotes, this creates massive temporary arrays. The vectorized approach is correct but could be optimized with: - Pre-computed coordinate grids - Smaller bounding boxes - Numba JIT compilation for the geometric calculations

docs.title: 3. Matrix Operation Overhead
docs.text:
In fitting.py:fit_first_and_second_harmonics(): 

A = np.column_stack([np.ones_like(phi), s1, c1, s2, c2])
coeffs, residuals, rank, s = np.linalg.lstsq(A, intensity, rcond=None)

Issue: For small sample sizes (common at small SMAs), np.linalg.lstsq overhead dominates. Could use: - Direct solving for 5x5 system: np.linalg.solve(A.T @ A, A.T @ intensity) - Cached matrix factorizations for fixed geometries

docs.title: 1. Configuration Complexity
docs.text:
Problem: The IsosterConfig validator has complex interdependencies: 

if self.integrator == 'adaptive' and self.lsb_sma_threshold is None:
    raise ValueError("lsb_sma_threshold must be provided when integrator='adaptive'")
if self.forced and (self.forced_sma is None or len(self.forced_sma) == 0):
    raise ValueError("forced_sma must be provided when forced=True")

Issue: This creates tight coupling between parameters and makes the configuration brittle. Adding new parameters requires updating the validator.

Suggestion: Use a builder pattern or separate validation groups.

docs.title: 2. Error Handling Inconsistencies
docs.text:
Silent Failures: Several functions return default values instead of raising exceptions: 

# In compute_parameter_errors()
except Exception:
    return 0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0  # Silent failure

Issue: This hides computational failures and can lead to misleading results.

Suggestion: Implement proper error types and propagate them up the call stack.

docs.title: 3. Memory Usage Patterns
docs.text:
Problem: In model.py:build_ellipse_model(), the model reconstruction creates large temporary arrays for each isophote:

y, x = np.mgrid[y_min:y_max, x_min:x_max]
# ... geometric calculations ...
model[y_min:y_max, x_min:x_max][mask] = intens

Issue: For large images with many isophotes, this causes significant memory allocation/deallocation overhead.

Fix: Pre-allocate working arrays and reuse them.

docs.title: 1. Vectorized Geometric Calculations
docs.text:
Current approach: 

for iso in sorted_isos:
    # Extract data for each isophote
    x_min, x_max, y_min, y_max = compute_bounds(iso)
    y, x = np.mgrid[y_min:y_max, x_min:x_max]
    # ... process ...

Optimized approach: 

# Batch process multiple isophotes
for batch in chunked_isophotes:
    # Extract all data at once
    # Process in vectorized batches

docs.title: 2. Cache Expensive Operations
docs.text:
Gradient computation could benefit from caching: 

# Cache coordinate transformations
@functools.lru_cache(maxsize=128)
def cached_ellipse_coordinates(x0, y0, sma, eps, pa, n_points):
    # Expensive geometric calculations

docs.title: 3. Pre-allocation Strategy
docs.text:
Model building should pre-allocate working arrays: 

def build_ellipse_model_optimized(image_shape, isophote_results):
    model = np.full(image_shape, fill)
    work_array = np.empty((max_height, max_width))  # Pre-allocate

    for iso in sorted_isos:
        # Reuse work_array instead of allocating new ones
        process_isophote(iso, model, work_array)

docs.title: Recommendations Priority
docs.text:
1. High Priority: Fix the EA mode inconsistency - this affects scientific accuracy
  2. High Priority: Eliminate redundant sampling in fit_isophote() - major performance gain
  3. Medium Priority: Improve error handling consistency
  4. Medium Priority: Optimize aperture photometry with pre-allocation
  5. Low Priority: Configuration refactoring for better maintainability

The codebase shows sophisticated understanding of the algorithm and good vectorization practices, but has some fundamental issues that could impact both performance and scientific accuracy.

docs.title: 2026-02-13 Phase 7 Plan: Documentation Integrity + Code Review
Goal
docs.text:
Execute a full documentation-and-code review pass focused on:

  1. Algorithm documentation fidelity (docs/spec.md, docs/algorithm.md)
  2. Stop-code correctness and doc consolidation decision (docs/stop-codes.md)
  3. docs/todo.md restructuring into active plan + short summary with archived progress references
  4. legacy optimization-notes migration to docs/future.md
  5. A careful bug/risk review with near-term and long-term outputs split across docs/todo.md and docs/future.md

docs.title: Constraints and Ground Rules
docs.text:
• Keep markdown names in lowercase kebab-case.
  • Keep edits minimal but complete; avoid speculative claims.
  • For every technical statement in docs, verify against current code before editing.
  • Do not guess metrics or performance claims; keep only measured/defensible statements.
  • Preserve finished historical details by moving them to journal/history references, not deleting project memory.

docs.title: Code modules to verify against docs
docs.text:
• isoster/driver.py
  • isoster/fitting.py
  • isoster/sampling.py
  • isoster/config.py
  • isoster/model.py
  • isoster/cog.py
  • isoster/utils.py
  • isoster/cli.py

docs.title: Documentation files to update
docs.text:
• docs/spec.md
  • docs/algorithm.md
  • docs/stop-codes.md
  • docs/user-guide.md (if stop-code merge target)
  • docs/todo.md
  • legacy optimization-notes page -> docs/future.md
  • docs/index.md
  • docs/index.md
  • mkdocs.yml
  • README.md and any other references to renamed docs

docs.title: Step 1: Establish truth baseline
docs.text:
• Extract current algorithmic behavior and stop-code generation paths from code.
  • Create a compact “docs claim vs code reality” matrix for:
  • fitting loop and convergence
  • sampling modes (PA vs eccentric anomaly)
  • geometry update policy
  • CoG/photometry semantics
  • template/forced modes
  • stop-code triggers and propagation

Deliverable:

  • Internal notes in working scratch (no final file required), used as source-of-truth for doc edits.

docs.title: Step 2: Audit and fix docs/spec.md + docs/algorithm.md
docs.text:
• Remove stale claims and replace with verified behavior.
  • Ensure parameter names and defaults match isoster/config.py.
  • Ensure algorithm equations/mapping to implementation are accurate.
  • Add explicit caveats where implementation differs from idealized math.

Deliverable:

  • Updated docs/spec.md
  • Updated docs/algorithm.md

Acceptance checks:

  • No claim remains that cannot be traced to current code.
  • Terms and symbols are consistent between spec and algorithm docs.

docs.title: Step 3: Audit docs/stop-codes.md and merge decision
docs.text:
• Verify each stop code against actual emitting logic in code paths.
  • Fix incorrect triggers, names, or recommendations.
  • Decide consolidation target based on maintainability:
  • merge into docs/algorithm.md if tightly implementation-specific
  • merge into docs/user-guide.md if primarily user-facing operations
  • Execute merge and de-duplicate text.

Deliverable:

  • Corrected stop-code content in final destination file.
  • If merged, leave docs/stop-codes.md as a short redirect note or remove and update links.

Acceptance checks:

  • One canonical stop-code reference location.
  • All repo links point to canonical location.

docs.title: Step 4: Restructure docs/todo.md and archive completed progress
docs.text:
• Create a compact progress summary file for completed historical phases in docs/journal/.
  • Add a 3-5 sentence summary at top of docs/todo.md describing what is completed.
  • Reference the new progress summary file at top of docs/todo.md.
  • Keep only active/pending tasks and near-term findings in docs/todo.md.

Deliverable:

  • New progress summary file under docs/journal/ (Phase 1-6 archive summary).
  • Updated top section of docs/todo.md with concise status + link.

Acceptance checks:

  • docs/todo.md is clearly actionable, not a full historical ledger.
  • Historical detail remains discoverable via referenced file.

docs.title: Step 5: Migrate legacy optimization notes to docs/future.md
docs.text:
• Rename file to docs/future.md.
  • Remove already-implemented items (for example existing JIT integration details).
  • Remove not-relevant or disproven ideas.
  • Keep only realistic, high-value future opportunities.
  • Split “future” content into:
  • long-term engineering upgrades
  • long-term algorithm/performance research directions

Deliverable:

  • docs/future.md (cleaned, scoped, current)
  • legacy optimization-notes content migrated

Acceptance checks:

  • No implemented work remains in future.md as future work.
  • No dead links to legacy optimization-notes page remain.

docs.title: Step 6: Conduct careful code review and capture findings
docs.text:
Review strategy:

  • Focus on bugs, correctness risks, robustness gaps, and missing validations.
  • Prioritize by severity and user impact.
  • Include concrete file references and, where possible, minimal fix direction.

Output split:

  • Short-term actionable issues -> add checklist entries to docs/todo.md
  • Long-term upgrades/optimizations -> add to docs/future.md

Deliverable:

  • Updated docs/todo.md with prioritized bug/risk/easy-win list.
  • Updated docs/future.md with long-term roadmap entries.

Acceptance checks:

  • Findings are specific, reproducible, and non-overlapping.
  • Short-term vs long-term boundary is clear.

docs.title: Step 7: Link integrity and minimal verification
docs.text:
• Update all references in docs and navigation files:
  • mkdocs.yml
  • docs/index.md
  • docs/index.md
  • README.md
  • any remaining internal links
  • Run quick consistency checks:
  • search for stale path references
  • optional markdown/link sanity scan if available

Deliverable:

  • clean link graph for renamed/merged docs

Acceptance checks:

  • no references to removed/renamed docs remain
  • docs navigation reflects canonical files

docs.title: Expected Final Artifacts
docs.text:
• Updated: docs/spec.md
  • Updated: docs/algorithm.md
  • Updated or merged: stop-code documentation
  • New: docs/future.md
  • Updated: docs/todo.md (active tasks + concise summary header)
  • New: docs/journal/<date>-phase1-6-progress-summary.md
  • Updated links: mkdocs.yml, docs/index.md, docs/index.md, README.md as needed

docs.title: Risks and Mitigations
docs.text:
• Risk: accidental loss of historical context while cleaning docs/todo.md.
  • Mitigation: archive first in journal summary before simplifying docs/todo.md.
  • Risk: doc claims drift from code during edits.
  • Mitigation: enforce claim-by-claim verification against source modules.
  • Risk: stop-code behavior may be subtle across driver/fitting interaction.
  • Mitigation: verify both origin and propagation paths before rewriting docs.

docs.title: What Was Done
docs.text:
• Reviewed active benchmark scripts and threshold policy:
  • benchmarks/performance/bench_efficiency.py
  • benchmarks/performance/bench_numba_speedup.py
  • benchmarks/performance/bench_vs_photutils.py
  • benchmarks/baselines/collect_phase4_profile_baseline.py

  • force mockgal.py benchmark/test workflows to use --engine libprofit
  • treat 2-D residual metrics as system-level diagnostics (extraction + model generation)
  • Updated planning docs:
  • docs/test-benchmark-improvement-plan.md (new 2026-02-14 session update section)
  • docs/todo.md (new Phase 9 checklist)
  • Renamed old output folder:
  • outputs/figures -> outputs/examples_ea_harmonics

docs.title: Key Decisions
docs.text:
• Keep threshold policy evidence-based: baseline measure first, then lock.
  • Separate metric interpretation:
  • 1-D metrics are primary for extraction algorithm accuracy.
  • 2-D metrics are system-level and may fail due to model reconstruction path.
  • mockgal integration must be explicit about backend (libprofit) to avoid backend drift.

  7. Run quick benchmark smoke then lock/update baseline files from measured outputs only.

docs.title: Post-Closeout Update: Efficiency Timing Jitter Handling
docs.text: After initial integration, full gate failures were traced to strict efficiency timing comparisons that did not account for measured per-case runtime variance.

docs.title: Policy Update
docs.text:
• Gate now applies measured jitter tolerance per efficiency case:
  • adjusted_threshold = locked_threshold + max(0.002s, 3.0 * current_std_time)
  • Configurable via:
  • --efficiency-time-jitter-sigma
  • --efficiency-time-jitter-floor-seconds
  • Policy parameters are written into benchmark_gate_report.json under gate_policy.

docs.title: Verification
docs.text:
• Quick gate with jitter policy:
  • outputs/benchmarks_performance/benchmark_gate_phase10_quick_qa_jitter/benchmark_gate_report.json
  • Result: Gate pass: True
  • Full gate with jitter policy (sequential run):
  • outputs/benchmarks_performance/benchmark_gate_full_refresh_phase10_qa_jitter_seq/benchmark_gate_report.json
  • Result: Gate pass: True

docs.title: Operational Note
docs.text: • Do not run multiple benchmark gate commands in parallel when collecting timing evidence; concurrent runs cause resource contention and invalidate runtime comparisons.

docs.title: Post-Closeout Update: Gate Defaults File
docs.text:
To avoid repeated CLI flags, gate runtime/QA defaults now come from:

  • benchmarks/baselines/benchmark_gate_defaults.json

The gate script supports optional override path:

  • --gate-defaults <path>

CLI-provided values still override file values.

docs.title: Problem Summary
docs.text: On large cases (for example NGC4767_mock1 at 4000x4000), campaign execution can appear stuck with no live progress visibility. Current logs are post-hoc and coarse, and there is no per-stage timeout or resume pointer.

docs.title: Goals
docs.text:
1. Add live visibility (--verbose) for stage/method start/end and errors.
  2. Add persistent per-stage logs (--save-log) with method-specific filenames.
  3. Add per-stage timeout control (--max-runtime-seconds, default 900).
  4. Add resumable execution (--continue-from at least galaxy-level).
  5. Add skip-by-default behavior with explicit --update override for reruns.
  6. Ensure QA stage respects extraction failures so failed methods do not trigger method QA/comparison work.

docs.title: A. QA extraction-status guard (correctness)
docs.text:
• Update run_huang2013_qa_afterburner.py to read extraction manifest (<prefix>_profiles_manifest.json by default).
  • If extraction status for a method is not success, mark method as skipped (reason=extraction_status_<status>) and do not run method QA rendering for that method.
  • Keep comparison QA disabled unless both methods are available and successful.

docs.title: B. Campaign runner observability and controls
docs.text:
• Refactor campaign extraction stage to run one subprocess per method when --method both.
  • Add options to run_huang2013_campaign.py:
  • --verbose
  • --save-log
  • --max-runtime-seconds (default 900)
  • --continue-from
  • --update
  • Implement streaming subprocess runner (Popen) with:
  • timeout handling,
  • optional live stdout/stderr emission,
  • optional log persistence.
  • Emit per-case/per-stage status in summary JSON (success, failed, timeout, skipped_existing).

docs.title: C. Single-image update mode support
docs.text:
• Add --update to run_huang2013_profile_extraction.py.
  • By default, skip method execution when output artifacts already exist and run JSON indicates prior success.
  • With --update, rerun and overwrite.

docs.title: D. Documentation and verification
docs.text:
• Update examples/huang2013/README.md with new options and resume examples.
  • Update docs/todo.md with checklist and verification evidence.
  • Run validation:
  • ruff check for modified scripts,
  • --help checks,
  • smoke runs for --continue-from, --update, and timeout path.

docs.title: Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
• During campaign run, operator can identify active stage/method in real time.
  • A hung method process is terminated at timeout and campaign continues.
  • Resume from --continue-from NGC4767 works without restarting earlier galaxies.
  • Default run skips completed outputs; --update forces rerun.
  • QA script does not run method QA/comparison for methods marked failed in extraction manifest.

docs.title: I3: Gradient formula incorrect in linear growth mode
docs.text:
• File: isoster/fitting.py:480
  • Problem: gradient = (mean_g - mean_c) / sma / step — for linear growth, delta_r = step (not sma * step), so dividing by sma is wrong.
  • Fix: delta_r = sma * step if not linear_growth else step; gradient = (mean_g - mean_c) / delta_r

docs.title: I5: PA interpolation wraps incorrectly at 0/180 boundary in model.py
docs.text:
• File: isoster/model.py:140
  • Problem: Linear interpolation of PA values that wrap (e.g., 170° to 10°) produces incorrect intermediate values (~90°).
  • Fix: Apply np.unwrap() to PA values before interpolation.

docs.text:

  • Added early guard in compute_parameter_errors before leastsq call (not just before var_residual) because scipy.optimize.leastsq itself raises TypeError when N_params > M_data.

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Investigate and fix excessive stop=2 (max-iterations-reached) in isoster's isophote fitting, first identified in NGC1209_mock2 from the Huang2013 full-sample campaign.

docs.title: Root Cause Analysis
docs.text:
• Identified that isoster's convergence check (|harmonic| < conver * rms) is SMA-independent, while photutils scales by sector_area which grows with SMA — outer isophotes converge much more easily in photutils
  • NGC1209_mock2 baseline: 9/66 stop=2 (12%) vs photutils 2/66 (3%)

docs.title: Three Convergence Improvements Implemented (branch: fix/convergence-stop2)
docs.text:
1. Convergence scaling (convergence_scaling): Scale threshold by approximate sector area or sqrt(SMA). Default changed to 'sector_area'. Commit 508bfae.
  2. Geometry damping (geometry_damping): Multiply geometry corrections by damping factor (0-1). Default 1.0 (no damping). Commit 096e01e.
  3. Geometry-change convergence (geometry_convergence): Secondary convergence when geometry stabilizes for N iterations. Default off. Commit 2cc1a65.

docs.title: Benchmark Results (NGC1209_mock2)
docs.text: Config stop=2 Time(s) Med dI/I Max dI/I Baseline 9 0.41 0.35% 26.8% A: sector_area 0 0.12 0.48% 11.8% A-alt: sqrt_sma 0 0.13 0.48% 11.8% B: damping=0.7 3 0.20 0.37% 16.3% C: geom_conv 3 0.22 0.40% 26.8% A+B combined 0 0.13 0.38% 18.9% A+C combined 0 0.13 0.48% 11.8%

  • 096e01e feat: implement geometry damping in fit_isophote

  • The sector_area scaling formula uses an approximation (not the exact photutils sector area computation which requires per-sample geometry). The approximation sma * delta_sma * angular_width may behave differently for edge cases.

docs.title: Key Decisions
docs.text:
• Default changed to convergence_scaling='sector_area' based on benchmark: eliminates all stop=2, 3.4x faster, better worst-case accuracy
  • geometry_damping default stays 1.0 (no damping) — useful supplementary option but not needed as default
  • geometry_convergence default stays False — helpful for edge cases but not universally needed
  • Vectorized sampling path untouched — all changes confined to fitting.py and config.py
  • The convergence scale factor is computed once before the iteration loop (since SMA is constant within a single isophote fit)

docs.text: Improve QA figures for the LegacySurvey high-order harmonics example, then review the harmonics implementation to understand why ESO243-49 and NGC3610 aren't benefiting from simultaneous_harmonics=True.

docs.title: 1. QA Figure Fixes (two quick fixes)
docs.text:
• Removed a4 panel from plot_qa_summary_extended in isoster/plotting.py: 7 panels → 6, redistributed height ratios
  • Removed a4 panel from plot_harmonic_comparison_qa in shared.py: 6 profile rows → 5

  • Built models on-the-fly via build_isoster_model (new optional condition_models parameter)

  • Regenerated QA figures for both galaxies — 160/160 tests pass

docs.title: 2. Harmonics Implementation Deep Review
docs.text:
• Key discovery: simultaneous_harmonics=True does NOT implement true ISOFIT (Ciambur 2015)
  • Higher-order harmonics (n≥3) are computed post-hoc only, never entering the iterative fitting loop
  • The iterative loop always uses only 5 parameters (I₀, A₁, B₁, A₂, B₂) regardless of settings
  • Three consequences: geometry bias from harmonic contamination, inflated RMS, post-hoc coefficients from biased geometry
  • Full review saved: docs/journal/2026-02-24-harmonics-review.md

docs.title: In Progress (Not Finished)
docs.text:
• Changes on feat/legacysurvey-harmonics-example are uncommitted
  • Next priority: Plan and implement true ISOFIT — integrate higher-order harmonics into the iterative loop when simultaneous_harmonics=True

docs.title: Problems / Blockers
docs.text: • None blocking. The harmonics review identifies a clear path forward.

docs.title: Key Decisions
docs.text:
• The fix requires modifying fit_isophote() to include all harmonic orders in the design matrix during iteration when simultaneous_harmonics=True
  • Geometry updates still use only A₁, B₁, A₂, B₂ (no change to update formulas)
  • RMS should be computed from residuals after removing ALL fitted harmonics
  • simultaneous_harmonics=False behavior must remain identical (backward compatible)

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Implement true ISOFIT (Ciambur 2015) — simultaneous harmonic fitting within the iteration loop when simultaneous_harmonics=True, replacing the post-hoc-only approach.

docs.title: Phase 1: New ISOFIT helper functions (DONE)
docs.text:
• build_isofit_design_matrix(angles, orders) — builds extended design matrix [1, sin(θ), cos(θ), sin(2θ), cos(2θ), sin(n₁θ), cos(n₁θ), ...]
  • fit_all_harmonics(angles, intens, orders) — simultaneous lstsq fit returning (coeffs, ata_inv)
  • evaluate_harmonic_model(angles, coeffs, orders) — evaluates full ISOFIT model for RMS

All added to isoster/fitting.py (before fit_higher_harmonics_simultaneous).

docs.title: Phase 1 tests (DONE)
docs.text: Six new unit tests in tests/unit/test_fitting.py: - test_build_isofit_design_matrix_shape - test_build_isofit_design_matrix_first_five_match - test_fit_all_harmonics_recovery (atol=1e-10) - test_fit_all_harmonics_insufficient_data - test_evaluate_harmonic_model_matches_5param - test_isofit_rms_cleaner_than_5param

docs.title: Phase 2: Modify fit_isophote() loop (DONE)
docs.text: Core changes in fit_isophote() in isoster/fitting.py: 1. Pre-loop setup: isofit_min_points (1 + 2(2+len(orders))), best_isofit_harmonics_stored flag 2. Per-iteration fallback: use_isofit_this_iter decided after sigma clipping. Falls back to 5-param when 6 <= points < isofit_min_points, with RuntimeWarning emitted once per isophote. 3. Dual-path harmonic fitting: fit_all_harmonics() vs fit_first_and_second_harmonics() 4. Dual-path model eval: evaluate_harmonic_model() vs harmonic_function() — ISOFIT RMS excludes higher-order signal 5. 5x5 covariance sub-matrix: cov_matrix[:5, :5] passed to compute_parameter_errors in ISOFIT mode 6. Harmonics stored during iteration: When use_isofit_this_iter and best_geometry updated, coefficients extracted and normalized by sma * |gradient|, errors from covariance diagonal 7. Post-convergence guards*: All three blocks (convergence, geometry-stability, max-iter) guarded by best_isofit_harmonics_stored

docs.title: Problems / Blockers
docs.text: None. Implementation is clean, all tests pass, real-data validation looks good.

docs.title: Key Decisions
docs.text:
1. No numba for ISOFIT path: Variable-width matrices negate JIT benefits; numpy lstsq is fast enough
  2. Per-iteration fallback: use_isofit_this_iter decided after sigma clipping (not pre-loop), so heavy masking mid-galaxy triggers fallback gracefully
  3. Harmonics stored during iteration: When best_geometry is updated and ISOFIT is active, coefficients are stored immediately (not just at convergence), so the best harmonics are always from the best geometry
  4. Post-hoc fallback: If ISOFIT couldn't activate (small SMA), the existing fit_higher_harmonics_simultaneous() post-hoc path is used as fallback
  5. Geometry updates unchanged: A1, B1, A2, B2 = coeffs[1:5] — same indexing in both paths, no geometry logic changes

docs.title: Model Reconstruction Audit
docs.text: Reviewed isoster/model.py build_isoster_model() and identified two issues:

docs.title: Issue 1: harmonic_orders default silently drops higher orders
docs.text:
• build_isoster_model() defaults harmonic_orders=[3, 4] (line 75 of model.py)
  • If fitting used harmonic_orders=[3,4,5,6,7], calling without explicit orders drops 5-7 silently
  • The example shared.py (lines 301-308) already works around this by auto-detecting from keys
  • Fix: Auto-detect available orders from isophote dict keys when harmonic_orders=None
  • Scan for keys matching pattern a{n} where n >= 3 in the first valid isophote

docs.title: Issue 2: EA mode angle space mismatch in model reconstruction
docs.text:
• When use_eccentric_anomaly=True, harmonics are fitted in ψ-space (eccentric anomaly)
  • But build_isoster_model() uses theta = arctan2(y_rot, x_rot) (line 206) which is φ (position angle)
  • Correct ψ would be arctan2(y_rot / (1 - eps), x_rot)
  • This is a pre-existing issue affecting both ISOFIT and post-hoc paths equally
  • Practical impact grows with ellipticity; matters most for eps > 0.5
  • Fix: Either:
  • (a) Add use_eccentric_anomaly parameter to build_isoster_model() and switch angle computation, OR
  • (b) Store the angle mode in the isophote results dict during fitting and auto-detect it

docs.title: Problems / Blockers
docs.text: None. Both issues are well-understood with clear fix paths.

docs.title: Key Decisions
docs.text:
1. Auto-detect pattern: Scan isophote keys for a{n} pattern rather than requiring explicit harmonic_orders parameter
  2. EA angle fix approach: Needs user input on whether to add a parameter or auto-detect from results

docs.title: Discovery: Harmonics Are Post-Hoc Only (Not True ISOFIT)
docs.text: The current simultaneous_harmonics=True option does not implement true ISOFIT (Ciambur 2015) behavior. Higher-order harmonics (n≥3) are computed as post-hoc diagnostics after convergence, never entering the iterative fitting loop.

docs.title: Phase 1 — Iterative geometry fitting (fit_isophote, lines 701-931)
docs.text:
• Fits ONLY 1st and 2nd order harmonics (5 parameters): I(θ) = I₀ + A₁sin(θ) + B₁cos(θ) + A₂sin(2θ) + B₂cos(2θ)
  • Uses A₁, B₁ to update center (x0, y0)
  • Uses A₂ to update position angle (pa)
  • Uses B₂ to update ellipticity (eps)
  • Convergence: max(|A₁|, |B₁|, |A₂|, |B₂|) < conver × scale × rms
  • RMS = std(data - 5-param model)

docs.title: Phase 2 — Post-hoc harmonic extraction (runs ONLY after Phase 1 converges)
docs.text:
• simultaneous_harmonics=False (default): compute_deviations() fits each order independently — I(θ) = c₀ + aₙsin(nθ) + bₙcos(nθ) per order n
  • simultaneous_harmonics=True: fit_higher_harmonics_simultaneous() fits all orders together: I(θ) = c₀ + Σₙ[aₙsin(nθ) + bₙcos(nθ)]
  • Both normalize coefficients by sma × |gradient| for dimensionless deviations

docs.title: 1. Higher-order signal contaminates geometry
docs.text: If a galaxy has strong a₄ (boxy/disky) structure, that signal is present during Phase 1. The 5-parameter model doesn't account for it, so higher-order power leaks into the RMS and can bias A₂/B₂ estimates (which control pa/eps). Cross-correlations between harmonic orders are ignored.

docs.title: 2. Inflated RMS affects convergence behavior
docs.text: Convergence tests harmonic amplitudes against conver × rms, but the RMS includes unmodeled higher-order structure. For galaxies with strong deviations from pure ellipses, the RMS is artificially high, making the threshold higher. The geometry converges but may be less accurate.

docs.title: 3. Post-hoc coefficients use biased geometry
docs.text: Since geometry (x0, y0, eps, pa) was fitted without accounting for higher-order terms, the angles/radii used for Phase 2 harmonic extraction may be slightly wrong.

docs.title: What True ISOFIT (Ciambur 2015) Does
docs.text:
ISOFIT fits ALL harmonics simultaneously within the iterative loop:

  1. Design matrix includes all orders: I(θ) = I₀ + A₁sin + B₁cos + A₂sin2 + B₂cos2 + a₃sin3 + b₃cos3 + a₄sin4 + b₄cos4 + ...
  2. Geometry updates still use only A₁, B₁, A₂, B₂ (same formulas)
  3. RMS is computed from residuals after removing all harmonic components → reflects truly random scatter
  4. A₁, B₁, A₂, B₂ are fitted jointly with higher orders → cross-correlations properly handled
  5. Convergence tested against this cleaner RMS

docs.title: Current Options Summary
docs.text:
Option What it controls Enters iterative loop? simultaneous_harmonics=False Each order fitted independently (post-hoc) No simultaneous_harmonics=True All orders fitted jointly (post-hoc) No harmonic_orders=[3,4] Which orders to compute No harmonic_orders=[3,4,5,6,7] More orders to compute No

None of these change the iterative fitting. The geometry loop always uses the same 5-parameter model.

docs.title: Why ESO243-49 and NGC3610 Aren't Benefiting
docs.text: These galaxies have strong non-elliptical structure (edge-on S0, boxy elliptical). The current approach correctly measures deviations (a₃, a₄, etc.) but doesn't account for them during fitting: - Geometry may be biased by harmonic contamination - Model reconstruction uses coefficients from a potentially biased geometry - Residuals still show structured patterns because coefficients aren't optimal

docs.title: Required Change: Integrate Harmonics Into the Iterative Loop
docs.text:
To implement true ISOFIT:

  1. Replace the 5-parameter fit_first_and_second_harmonics() inside the iteration loop with a joint fit of all requested orders (1, 2, 3, 4, ...) when simultaneous_harmonics=True
  2. Compute RMS from residuals after removing ALL fitted harmonics
  3. Still use only A₁, B₁, A₂, B₂ for geometry updates (no change to update formulas)
  4. Test convergence against the cleaned RMS
  5. Store all harmonic coefficients at each iteration (not just at convergence)

docs.title: Key Functions to Modify
docs.text:
• fit_isophote() in fitting.py — main iteration loop
  • fit_first_and_second_harmonics() → generalize to fit N orders simultaneously
  • Convergence test (line 834) — use cleaned RMS
  • best_geometry tracking — store harmonics during iteration, not only at convergence

docs.title: Design Considerations
docs.text:
• When simultaneous_harmonics=False, behavior should remain identical (backward compatible)
  • When simultaneous_harmonics=True, the design matrix grows: 5 params → 1 + 2*len(harmonic_orders) + 4 (intercept + 2 per higher order + 4 for orders 1,2)
  • Need enough data points: n_samples > n_params (already checked in fit_higher_harmonics_simultaneous)
  • The compute_deviations() sequential function becomes unnecessary when using true simultaneous mode, but should be kept for backward compatibility

docs.title: Related Files
docs.text:
• isoster/fitting.py — core fitting loop and harmonic functions
  • isoster/sampling.py — data extraction (no changes needed)
  • isoster/model.py — model reconstruction (no changes needed, uses same coefficients)
  • isoster/driver.py — orchestration (no changes needed)
  • isoster/config.py — may need new config option to distinguish legacy vs true ISOFIT mode

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Implemented true ISOFIT (Ciambur 2015) simultaneous harmonic fitting within the fit_isophote() iteration loop. When simultaneous_harmonics=True, higher-order harmonics are now fitted jointly with geometry harmonics (orders 1-2) using an extended design matrix, rather than being computed post-hoc after convergence.

docs.title: New Functions in fitting.py
docs.text:
• build_isofit_design_matrix(angles, orders) — extended design matrix
  • fit_all_harmonics(angles, intens, orders) — simultaneous lstsq fit
  • evaluate_harmonic_model(angles, coeffs, orders) — full ISOFIT model evaluation

docs.title: Modified fit_isophote() Loop
docs.text:
• Per-iteration use_isofit_this_iter flag after sigma clipping
  • Dual-path fitting: fit_all_harmonics() vs fit_first_and_second_harmonics()
  • ISOFIT RMS excludes higher-order signal (cleaner convergence criterion)
  • 5x5 covariance sub-matrix for geometry error estimation in ISOFIT mode
  • Harmonics stored at best-geometry update during iteration
  • Post-convergence guards: skip post-hoc if ISOFIT already stored harmonics
  • Fallback with RuntimeWarning when n_points < 1 + 2*(2 + len(orders))

docs.title: Design Decisions
docs.text:
1. No numba for ISOFIT path — variable-width matrices negate JIT benefits
  2. Per-iteration fallback — decided after sigma clipping, not pre-loop
  3. Harmonics stored during iteration — at best-geometry update, not only at convergence
  4. Geometry updates unchanged — A1, B1, A2, B2 = coeffs[1:5] in both paths

docs.title: Unit Tests (Phase 1-2): 11 new tests
docs.text:

  • 5 behavior tests (default path unchanged, convergence, fallback, mixed mode)

docs.title: Integration Tests (Phase 3): 4 new tests
docs.text:
• Boxy Sersic recovery: median |a4,b4| = 0.043 vs injected 0.04
  • ISOFIT vs post-hoc: b4 correlation = 1.000, identical median magnitudes
  • M51 regression: no convergence degradation (55.6% vs 66.7%)
  • EA + ISOFIT: 100% convergence on eps=0.6 mock, 0.3% intensity accuracy

docs.title: Full Suite
docs.text: 175/175 tests pass, zero regressions.

docs.title: NGC3610 high-order harmonic exploration
docs.text:
• Created examples/ngc3610_highorder_exploration.py — two investigations:
  • Harmonic impact on model: Confirmed harmonics are working correctly. ~14.6% RMS improvement with harmonics vs without. Clear structured correction pattern visible in 2D difference maps.
  • Convergence criteria: Tested default (conver=0.05/maxit=50), strict (0.01/200), very strict (0.005/500). All three produce essentially identical RMS (~0.0727). Stricter criteria lose more convergences while burning 6x more iterations. Default is adequate.
  • Output: 11 figures in outputs/ngc3610_highorder_exploration/

docs.title: NGC3610 mask effect exploration
docs.text:
• Created examples/ngc3610_mask_effect.py — masked vs no-mask comparison
  • Key finding: the 32% object mask has subtle systematic effects on geometry at outer radii, but overall impact is small (RMS 0.07266 vs 0.07269). High-order harmonics are sensitive to image details regardless of whether features belong to the target.
  • Output: 3 figures in outputs/ngc3610_highorder_exploration/

docs.title: In Progress (Not Finished)
docs.text:
• All changes remain uncommitted on feat/isofit-mode-comparison
  • Next session: explore the effect of initial SMA (sma0 / inisma) values on NGC3610 fitting
  • May want to commit the exploration scripts and isofit_mode changes after the inisma investigation

docs.title: Problems / Blockers
docs.text:
• ESO243-49 fractional residual stats show 100% median due to near-zero outskirt pixels (metric limitation for edge-on galaxies)
  • Remaining structured residuals in NGC3610 are real morphological features — not addressable by convergence tuning, harmonic orders, or mask changes

docs.title: Key Decisions & Lessons
docs.text:
• High-order harmonics [3-7] provide measurable (~15%) model improvement over no-harmonics, confirming model reconstruction is correct
  • Default convergence settings are sufficient — tighter criteria don't improve the model
  • Object mask effect is subtle and systematic but not large; harmonics are sensitive to all image details
  • Principle: Always inspect 2D residual patterns, not just scalar RMS — the compression from 2D to 1D is inherently lossy
  • Principle: Don't draw conclusions from small-N changes (e.g., 2 vs 1 stop=2 failures is not meaningful)

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Add an isofit_mode config parameter to compare the original Ciambur 2015 ISOFIT algorithm (post-hoc simultaneous harmonics) against isoster's current in-loop variant, and run a quantitative comparison on LegacySurvey galaxies.

docs.title: 1. Config: isofit_mode parameter
docs.text:
• Added isofit_mode: str = 'in_loop' | 'original' to IsosterConfig
  • Only meaningful when simultaneous_harmonics=True

docs.title: 2. Fitting: "original" mode in fit_isophote()
docs.text:
• isofit_mode='original': uses 5-param inside the loop (identical to default), then fits all higher-order harmonics simultaneously post-hoc after convergence
  • Saves best_angles, best_intens, best_gradient at best-geometry snapshot for accurate post-hoc fitting
  • Three post-hoc exit points updated (convergence, geometry convergence, max-iter)

docs.title: 3. Unit tests (3 new, 182 total)
docs.text:
• test_isofit_original_mode_geometry_matches_default: confirms geometry, RMS, niter, stop_code are bitwise identical to default 5-param
  • test_isofit_original_mode_stores_harmonics: verifies post-hoc harmonics are nonzero
  • test_isofit_original_differs_from_in_loop: both modes produce valid, different results

docs.title: 4. Comparison script
docs.text:
• examples/compare_isofit_modes.py — runs ESO243-49 and NGC3610 with 3 configs
  • Generates QA figures with 2D residual maps + 6-panel 1D profiles

docs.title: Convergence confirms implementation correctness
docs.text:
• Baseline and original have identical iteration counts and geometry (both use 5-param in loop)
  • In-loop mode has slightly different convergence due to lower RMS from full-model subtraction

docs.title: ESO243-49 (edge-on S0)
docs.text:
Mode Converged Mean Iter Baseline 60/60 11.1 ISOFIT in-loop 58/60 12.8 ISOFIT original 60/60 11.1
  • In-loop loses 2 isophotes — the lower ISOFIT RMS makes convergence criterion harder to satisfy relative to the rescaled threshold
  • 2D residual patterns visually identical across modes

docs.title: NGC3610 (boxy elliptical)
docs.text:
| Mode | Converged | Mean Iter | Mid |res|% | Mid rms% | |------|-----------|-----------|---------|---------| | Baseline | 60/60 | 10.9 | 6.87 | 13.68 | | ISOFIT in-loop | 60/60 | 11.3 | 6.72 | 13.34 | | ISOFIT original | 60/60 | 10.9 | 6.79 | 13.62 |

  • In-loop gives marginally better mid-region residuals
  • Differences in a4/b4 visible at outer radii

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text: The structured residual patterns in both galaxies are not caused by the ISOFIT algorithm variant. All three modes produce nearly identical 2D residuals. The residual structure likely comes from: 1. Real morphological features not captured by low-order harmonics 2. Masking artifacts (both galaxies have significant masked regions) 3. Model reconstruction interpolation effects

docs.title: Bug: R26-01
docs.text: Second-gradient formula in compute_gradient (fitting.py:642-645) is wrong for linear growth mode. The first gradient was fixed in I3, but the second-gradient fallback path still divides by sma * 2 * step instead of 2 * step when linear_growth=True. Off by a factor of sma. Low practical impact (linear growth rarely used, geometric is default).

docs.title: Design Issue: R26-05 (Forced Photometry API)
docs.text:
The current API has two overlapping mechanisms: 1. forced=True + forced_sma=[...] — single fixed geometry, nearly useless for real galaxies 2. template_isophotes=[...] — variable geometry per SMA, the actually useful workflow

Proposed unification: replace forced/forced_sma config fields with a single template parameter on fit_image() that accepts: - A file path (string) — loads via isophote_results_from_fits() - A results dict — extracts ['isophotes'] - A list of isophote dicts — current behavior

This eliminates the confusing dual mechanism and makes the common multiband workflow a one-liner.

docs.title: Code Quality: R26-03
docs.text: Post-hoc harmonic deviation block is copy-pasted 3 times in fit_isophote (~60 lines duplicated). Should be extracted to a helper function.

docs.title: ISOSTER Code Review and Improvement Roadmap
Executive Summary
docs.text: This document presents a critical review of the ISOSTER codebase, identifying 4 critical bugs, 8 high-priority issues, and 15+ medium/low priority improvements. The codebase is generally well-structured with good vectorization, but has significant issues in error handling, API consistency, and test coverage.

docs.title: BUG-1: Incorrect PA Update Formula (CRITICAL)
docs.text:
File: isoster/fitting.py:593-595 

denom = ((1.0 - eps)**2 - 1.0)  # Always negative for eps ∈ [0,1)!
if denom == 0: denom = 1e-6    # Unreachable
pa = (pa + (max_amp * 2.0 * (1.0 - eps) / sma / gradient / denom)) % np.pi
Problem: Since (1-eps)² ∈ (0,1], the denominator is always negative. The if denom == 0 check is unreachable. The PA update has incorrect sign. Impact: Geometry fitting converges to wrong PA values. Fix: Use denom = 1.0 - (1.0 - eps)**2 or -eps * (2 - eps).

docs.title: BUG-2: API Breaking Change - Return Type Mismatch (CRITICAL)
docs.text:
File: isoster/sampling.py:172-185 vs tests/test_sampling.py:37 

# Function returns 4-element IsophoteData namedtuple
return IsophoteData(angles=..., phi=..., intens=..., radii=...)

# Test expects 3-element tuple
phi, intens, radii = extract_isophote_data(...)  # ValueError!
Impact: Test suite fails. Breaking change if external code uses tuple unpacking.

docs.title: BUG-3: Missing None Check Causes TypeError (CRITICAL)
docs.text:
File: isoster/fitting.py:515 

gradient_relative_error = abs(gradient_error / gradient) if (gradient_error is not None and gradient < 0) else None
Problem: If gradient is None, comparison gradient < 0 raises TypeError. Fix: Add gradient is not None and before gradient < 0.

docs.title: BUG-4: Division by Zero in Parameter Errors (CRITICAL)
docs.text:
File: isoster/fitting.py:230-239 

ea = abs(errors[2] / gradient)  # No zero check
Problem: Divides by gradient without checking for zero. Silent failure via bare except Exception.

docs.title: ISSUE-1: Bare except Exception Handlers
docs.text: Files: fitting.py:244, 281 - Silently returns (0, 0, 0, 0) masking real errors - Fix: Catch specific exceptions, add logging

docs.title: ISSUE-2: Unused Numba Import
docs.text:
File: sampling.py:3 

from numba import njit  # Never used
- Either use @njit on hot loops or remove import

docs.title: ISSUE-3: Dead Code File
docs.text: File: isoster/optimize_backup.py (entire file) - Complete backup of old implementation - Fix: Remove or move to reference/

docs.title: ISSUE-4: Integer Truncation for Central Pixel
docs.text:
File: driver.py:20,23 

val = image[int(y0), int(x0)]  # Truncates, should round
- Fix: Use int(np.round(y0)), int(np.round(x0))

docs.title: ISSUE-5: Inconsistent Stop Codes
docs.text:
• extract_forced_photometry() returns -1 for no data
  • fit_isophote() returns 1 for same condition
  • Fix: Document and standardize stop code meanings

docs.title: ISSUE-6: Incomplete Test Coverage
docs.text: Missing tests for: - fit_image() main entry point - Forced mode, EA mode, CoG mode - Edge cases (empty images, all-masked) - Config validation

docs.title: ISSUE-7: Long Function Signatures
docs.text: File: fitting.py:284 - compute_gradient() has 12 parameters - Fix: Group into geometry/config dataclasses

docs.title: ISSUE-8: Inconsistent Parameter Ordering
docs.text:
extract_isophote_data(image, mask, x0, y0, sma, eps, pa, ...)
extract_forced_photometry(image, mask, sma, x0, y0, eps, pa, ...)  # Different!

docs.title: EFF-1: Redundant Gradient Extractions
docs.text: File: fitting.py:284-354 - Extracts isophote data at up to 3 SMAs per call - Early termination could skip unnecessary extractions - Impact: ~3x overhead in gradient computation

docs.title: EFF-2: Repeated Harmonic Fits
docs.text: File: fitting.py:195-245 - compute_parameter_errors() refits harmonics even when coefficients available - Fix: Pass coeffs as parameter

docs.title: EFF-3: Unused Coordinate Grid Allocation
docs.text:
File: model.py:29 

yy, xx = np.mgrid[:h, :w]  # Never used!
- Wastes ~32MB for 4096x4096 image

docs.title: EFF-4: Repeated Trig Computations
docs.text: File: sampling.py:152-153 - np.cos(pa), np.sin(pa) recomputed for each SMA - Cache at driver level for 100+ isophotes

docs.title: EFF-5: PA Wrap-Around Using While Loops
docs.text:
File: fitting.py:44-48 

while delta_pa > np.pi:
    delta_pa -= 2 * np.pi
- Fix: Use ((delta_pa + np.pi) % (2*np.pi)) - np.pi

docs.title: API-1: Missing CLI Options
docs.text: Current CLI lacks: --debug, --max_sma, --min_sma, --integrator, --compute_cog, --conver

docs.title: API-2: Magic Numbers Not Configurable
docs.text:
• 64 minimum samples (sampling.py:127)
  • 0.8 gradient fallback multiplier (fitting.py:295)
  • 1e-6 regularization cutoff (fitting.py:35)

docs.title: API-3: Inconsistent Result Dict Fields
docs.text:
• Some fields only present with certain config options
  • Mix of 0.0 and np.nan for missing values
  • No dataclass/schema for result structure

docs.title: API-4: Missing Docstrings
docs.text:
• harmonic_function(), main(), load_config() lack docstrings
  • Incomplete parameter descriptions in several functions

docs.title: API-5: PA Modulo Bug
docs.text:
File: fitting.py:595 

pa = (...) % np.pi  # Should be % (2 * np.pi)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Create comprehensive benchmark tests comparing isoster vs photutils.isophote using synthetic Sersic profile models.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Create new branch before any edits: git checkout -b benchmarks/sersic-comparison
  2. Stay in branch until explicitly permitted to merge

docs.title: 6.1 Sersic Model Generation
docs.text:
File to create: benchmarks/benchmark_sersic_accuracy.py

Model Parameters: | Parameter | Values | |-----------|--------| | Sersic index (n) | 1.0, 2.0, 4.0 | | Effective radius (Re) | 20, 50, 100 pixels | | Ellipticity (eps) | 0.0, 0.3, 0.6 | | Position angle (PA) | 0, π/4, π/2 radians | | Image size | 512×512 pixels |

Central Region Handling: - Use oversampling (e.g., 10×10 subpixels) for pixels within 3× the PSF FWHM equivalent (~3 pixels) - Average subpixel values to get final pixel value - No PSF convolution

Noise Variants: 1. Noiseless (clean) 2. Gaussian noise with S/N = 100 at Re 3. Gaussian noise with S/N = 50 at Re

docs.title: 6.2 True 1D Sersic Profile
docs.text:
Compute analytically: 

def sersic_1d(r, I_e, R_e, n):
    """True Sersic intensity profile."""
    b_n = 1.9992 * n - 0.3271  # Approximation for b_n
    return I_e * np.exp(-b_n * ((r / R_e)**(1/n) - 1))

docs.title: 6.3 Comparison Metrics
docs.text:
For each test case, compare:

Metric isoster photutils True 1D Intensity profile I(sma) ✓ ✓ ✓ Ellipticity profile eps(sma) ✓ ✓ constant Position angle profile PA(sma) ✓ ✓ constant Runtime (seconds) ✓ ✓ N/A

docs.title: 6.4 Acceptance Criteria
docs.text: Criterion Noiseless With Noise SMA range for accuracy check 1×Re to 5×Re (or 5px to 5×Re if Re<5px) 1×Re to 3×Re Max intensity deviation 0% (within numerical precision) Statistical consistency Max eps deviation 0% Statistical consistency Max PA deviation 0% Statistical consistency isoster speedup vs photutils >4× >4×

docs.title: 1. Objective
docs.text:
• Main goal:
  • Harden Huang2013 campaign fault tolerance and address ESO185-G054 failure classification issues.
  • Scope for next session:
  • Run and review updated real outputs for ESO185-G054 under ~/work/hsc/huang2013/ESO185-G054.

docs.title: 2. Current Status
docs.text:
• Completed:
  • Added method success gate based on profile quality (isophote_count >= 3) in extraction/campaign reusable checks.
  • Added extraction failure classification for insufficient profile outputs and failed run_json persistence.
  • Added QA warnings for large inter-method count mismatch (abs(count_photutils - count_isoster) >= 10).
  • Added artifact availability warnings and surfaced them in QA manifest and _report.md.
  • Added Huang2013-specific initialization fix: hard-coded PA - 90 deg in infer_initial_geometry.
  • Changed missing RE_PX1 fallback from 8.0 to 6.0 pixels (still clamped to >= 3.0).
  • Updated examples/huang2013/README.md and docs/lessons.md.
  • Added/updated regression tests in tests/unit/test_huang2013_campaign_fault_tolerance.py.
  • In progress:
  • User requested real run refresh for ESO185-G054 to inspect updated behavior on production outputs.
  • Blockers:
  • None.

docs.title: 3. Key References
docs.text:
• examples/huang2013/run_huang2013_real_mock_demo.py: initialization logic (PA - 90 deg, RE_PX1 fallback 6.0).
  • examples/huang2013/run_huang2013_profile_extraction.py: extraction success gate and failed-run payload persistence.
  • examples/huang2013/run_huang2013_campaign.py: reusable-success gate in campaign orchestration.
  • examples/huang2013/run_huang2013_qa_afterburner.py: comparison mismatch warning + artifact availability warnings.
  • examples/huang2013/README.md: Huang2013 workflow notes with new initialization conventions.
  • tests/unit/test_huang2013_campaign_fault_tolerance.py: regression coverage for these fixes.

docs.title: 4. Decisions
docs.text:
• Decision:
  • Use isophote_count >= 3 as success threshold for extracted profile quality.
  • Rationale:

  • >0 is too permissive and can classify nearly unusable outputs as successful.

  • Decision:

  • Hard-code Huang2013 PA initialization correction (PA_init = PA_header - 90 deg) in Huang2013 helper code.
  • Rationale:

  • Empirically observed convention mismatch on ESO185-G054; scope is Huang2013-specific workflow.

  • Decision:

  • Use RE_PX1 fallback 6.0 pixels (instead of 8.0) when missing.
  • Rationale:
  • Requested project default update; still keep minimum clamp at 3.0 for robustness.

docs.title: 4. Decisions
docs.text:
• Decision: enforce non-negative error values inside the core isoster run path (not just scripts).

  • Decision: for Huang2013 scripts, use fixed default sma0=6.0 px rather than RE_PX1-based fallback.
  • Rationale: aligns with current campaign policy and avoids header-dependent initialization drift.

docs.text:

    • cog column itself is finite for all rows.
  • Implemented CoG harmonization fallback in QA/data-prep path (method_cog_flux -> cog -> tflux_e -> true_cog_flux) and recompute cog_rel_diff.
  • Regenerated isoster-only QA for mock3 at:

docs.title: 4. Decisions
docs.text:
• Decision: enforce method-specific 2-D model reconstruction in QA pipelines.
  • Rationale: model builder should match extraction method; previously photutils profiles were reconstructed with isoster model code.
  • Decision: harmonize method_cog_flux with fallback precedence to avoid QA point loss when tflux_e is sparse.
  • Rationale: CoG in QA should have one-to-one valid correspondence with finite 1-D profile rows when CoG information exists.

  • outputs/huang2013_mock3_stopcode2_compare/ESO185-G054_mock3_stopcode2_side_by_side_report.md: direct comparison report for stop-code/CoG behavior.

docs.title: 4. Decisions
docs.text:
• Decision: use explicit stop_code=2 in isoster for max-iteration fallback (same semantic bucket as photutils).
  • Rationale: prevents silent stop_code=0 mislabeling on non-converged rows and cleanly distinguishes convergence from max-iteration fallback.
  • Decision: treat stop_code=2 as acceptable in regular growth propagation.
  • Rationale: keeps behavior consistent with “valid but non-converged fallback geometry” use case and with photutils-style continuation.

docs.title: Progress
docs.text:
• Identified the mock3 comparison QA crash root cause: photutils row with intens < 0 produced negative sb_err_mag, which broke matplotlib errorbar(yerr=...).
  • Updated Huang2013 profile preparation to compute sb_mag_arcsec2 and sb_err_mag only for intens > 0; non-positive intensity rows now keep both values as NaN.
  • Added extraction-time validation for all error columns (*_err, *_error): any negative value now marks the method run as failed and writes warning/validation metadata into run JSON and manifests.
  • Updated QA afterburner to carry extraction warnings from method run JSON into QA manifest/report warnings.
  • Changed Huang2013 initial SMA behavior to fixed default sma0 = 6.0 pixels (not RE_PX1-based fallback).
  • Updated docs/rules to persist the fixed-6-pixel SMA rule (CLAUDE.md, examples/huang2013/README.md).
  • Expanded regression tests in tests/unit/test_huang2013_campaign_fault_tolerance.py; targeted suite passed: 8 passed.
  • Re-ran ESO185-G054 campaign in --update mode; all 4 mocks completed extraction and QA successfully for both methods, with 4 comparison QA figures generated.
  • Confirmed all regenerated ESO185-G054 run JSON files report fit_config.sma0 = 6.0.
  • Committed and merged to main (fast-forward): commit ae1c6ae.

docs.title: Lessons Learned
docs.text:
• Surface-brightness quantities must be explicitly undefined when intensity is non-positive; propagating computed errors from invalid intensity causes downstream plotting failures.
  • Error-sign validation should be centralized at extraction output boundaries so invalid uncertainties are caught before QA/report generation.
  • Comparison QA plotting should apply finite/non-negative masks on both value and error arrays even when upstream validation exists.

docs.title: Convergence stop=2 Fix — Implementation Plan
docs.text:
For Claude: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

Goal: Reduce stop=2 (max-iterations-reached) rate to near-zero in well-defined regions by implementing three convergence improvements, benchmarking them on NGC1209_mock2, and selecting the best combination.

Architecture: Three independent convergence improvements to isoster/fitting.py, each gated by new optional config params in isoster/config.py. A standalone diagnostic script compares baseline vs each approach on NGC1209_mock2. The vectorized sampling path (sampling.py) is NOT modified.

Tech Stack: numpy, scipy, pydantic (config), astropy (FITS I/O), matplotlib (QA figures)

Branch: fix/convergence-stop2

docs.title: Root cause
docs.text:
Isoster's convergence check (fitting.py:819): 

abs(max_amp) < conver * rms
is SMA-independent. Photutils (reference/fitter.py:199) uses: 
conver * sector_area * std(residual) > abs(largest_harmonic)
where sector_area grows with SMA, relaxing the threshold for outer isophotes.

docs.title: NGC1209_mock2 baseline
docs.text:
• 8/66 isophotes (12%) hit stop=2, all at SMA > 95 px
  • Photutils: 2/66 (3%) stop=2
  • Photometry is accurate (~1%) despite stop=2 — geometry oscillates

docs.title: Constraint
docs.text:
• Must NOT change sampling.py or the vectorized sampling path
  • Must NOT degrade accuracy (intensity, eps, pa) on existing test suite
  • New params must have good defaults so existing users see improvement automatically

docs.title: Task 1: Add convergence scaling config params
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: isoster/config.py:34-37 (fitting control section)

Step 1: Add three new config fields

Add after conver (line 37):

convergence_scaling: str = Field(
    default='sector_area',
    pattern='^(none|sector_area|sqrt_sma)$',
    description="Scale convergence threshold with SMA. "
                "'sector_area': multiply by approximate sector area (matches photutils behavior). "
                "'sqrt_sma': multiply by sqrt(sma). "
                "'none': constant threshold (legacy behavior)."
)

geometry_damping: float = Field(
    default=1.0,
    gt=0.0,
    le=1.0,
    description="Damping factor for geometry updates (0 < d <= 1). "
                "Each geometry correction is multiplied by this factor. "
                "1.0 = no damping (legacy). 0.5 = half-step corrections. "
                "Lower values prevent oscillations but slow convergence."
)

geometry_convergence: bool = Field(
    default=False,
    description="Enable secondary convergence based on geometry stability. "
                "Declares convergence when geometry changes fall below tolerance "
                "for consecutive iterations, even if harmonic criterion is not met."
)

geometry_tolerance: float = Field(
    default=0.01,
    gt=0.0,
    description="Threshold for geometry convergence. Convergence declared when "
                "max(|delta_eps|, |delta_pa/pi|, |delta_x0/sma|, |delta_y0/sma|) "
                "< geometry_tolerance for geometry_stable_iters consecutive iterations."
)

geometry_stable_iters: int = Field(
    default=3,
    ge=2,
    description="Number of consecutive iterations with small geometry changes "
                "required to trigger geometry-based convergence."
)

Step 2: Run test collection to verify config is valid

Run: uv run pytest tests/ --collect-only -q 2>&1 | tail -5 Expected: all tests collected, no import errors

Step 3: Commit

git add isoster/config.py
git commit -m "feat: add convergence scaling, geometry damping, and geometry convergence config params"

docs.title: Task 2: Implement convergence scaling (Approach A) in fitting.py
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: isoster/fitting.py (convergence check at line 819 and surrounding context)

Step 1: Write the failing test

File: tests/unit/test_convergence.py

"""Tests for convergence improvements in fit_isophote."""
import numpy as np
import pytest
from isoster.fitting import fit_isophote
from isoster.config import IsosterConfig


def make_sersic_image(shape=(201, 201), x0=100, y0=100, ie=1000.0, re=30.0,
                      n=2.0, eps=0.3, pa=0.5):
    """Create a noiseless Sersic galaxy image for testing convergence."""
    from scipy.special import gammaincinv
    bn = gammaincinv(2 * n, 0.5)
    yy, xx = np.mgrid[:shape[0], :shape[1]]
    dx = xx - x0
    dy = yy - y0
    cos_pa, sin_pa = np.cos(pa), np.sin(pa)
    x_rot = dx * cos_pa + dy * sin_pa
    y_rot = -dx * sin_pa + dy * cos_pa
    r_ellip = np.sqrt(x_rot**2 + (y_rot / (1 - eps))**2)
    image = ie * np.exp(-bn * ((r_ellip / re)**(1.0/n) - 1))
    return image


class TestConvergenceScaling:
    """Test that sector_area scaling helps outer isophotes converge."""

    def test_outer_isophote_converges_with_scaling(self):
        """An outer isophote that hits maxit with 'none' should converge with 'sector_area'."""
        image = make_sersic_image(shape=(401, 401), x0=200, y0=200, re=30.0, eps=0.3)
        mask = np.zeros_like(image, dtype=bool)
        start_geom = {'x0': 200.0, 'y0': 200.0, 'eps': 0.3, 'pa': 0.5}

        # Use a tight conver to force stop=2 at large SMA with no scaling
        cfg_none = IsosterConfig(conver=0.02, maxit=30, minit=5,
                                 convergence_scaling='none')
        result_none = fit_isophote(image, mask, 150.0, start_geom, cfg_none)

        # With sector_area scaling, same params should converge
        cfg_scaled = IsosterConfig(conver=0.02, maxit=30, minit=5,
                                   convergence_scaling='sector_area')
        result_scaled = fit_isophote(image, mask, 150.0, start_geom, cfg_scaled)

        # The scaled version should converge (stop=0) or at least use fewer iterations
        assert result_scaled['niter'] <= result_none['niter'], \
            f"Scaling should help: {result_scaled['niter']} vs {result_none['niter']}"

    def test_scaling_none_preserves_legacy(self):
        """convergence_scaling='none' should match legacy behavior exactly."""
        image = make_sersic_image()
        mask = np.zeros_like(image, dtype=bool)
        start_geom = {'x0': 100.0, 'y0': 100.0, 'eps': 0.3, 'pa': 0.5}

        cfg = IsosterConfig(convergence_scaling='none')
        result = fit_isophote(image, mask, 30.0, start_geom, cfg)
        # Just verify it runs and returns valid result
        assert result['stop_code'] in {0, 1, 2, 3, -1}
        assert result['sma'] == 30.0

    def test_sqrt_sma_scaling(self):
        """sqrt_sma scaling should also help outer isophotes."""
        image = make_sersic_image(shape=(401, 401), x0=200, y0=200, re=30.0, eps=0.3)
        mask = np.zeros_like(image, dtype=bool)
        start_geom = {'x0': 200.0, 'y0': 200.0, 'eps': 0.3, 'pa': 0.5}

        cfg = IsosterConfig(conver=0.02, maxit=30, minit=5,
                            convergence_scaling='sqrt_sma')
        result = fit_isophote(image, mask, 150.0, start_geom, cfg)
        assert result['stop_code'] in {0, 1, 2, 3, -1}

Step 2: Run test to verify it fails

Run: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_convergence.py -v Expected: FAIL (convergence_scaling not recognized or no effect yet)

Step 3: Implement convergence scaling in fit_isophote

In fitting.py, inside fit_isophote(), just before the convergence check at line 819:

# Compute convergence scaling factor based on config
convergence_scaling = cfg.convergence_scaling
if convergence_scaling == 'sector_area':
    # Approximate sector area: proportional to sma * delta_sma * angular_width
    # n_samples = max(64, int(2*pi*sma)), angular_width ~ 2*pi/n_samples
    # delta_sma ~ sma * astep (geometric) or astep (linear)
    # sector_area ~ sma * (sma * astep) * (2*pi / n_samples) ~ sma * astep * 2*pi * sma / n_samples
    # For geometric stepping: ~ sma * astep (since n_samples ~ 2*pi*sma)
    # Simplify: scale_factor proportional to sma * astep, normalized to 1.0 at sma=1
    n_samples = max(64, int(2 * np.pi * sma))
    angular_width = 2 * np.pi / n_samples
    astep = cfg.astep
    delta_sma = sma * astep if not cfg.linear_growth else astep
    scale_factor = max(1.0, sma * delta_sma * angular_width)
elif convergence_scaling == 'sqrt_sma':
    scale_factor = max(1.0, np.sqrt(sma))
else:  # 'none'
    scale_factor = 1.0

Then modify the convergence check:

if abs(max_amp) < conver * scale_factor * rms and i >= minit:

Step 4: Run tests

Run: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_convergence.py -v Expected: PASS

Step 5: Run existing test suite to verify no regressions

Run: uv run pytest tests/unit/ tests/integration/ tests/validation/ -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tail -20 Expected: 152 tests pass

Step 6: Commit

git add isoster/fitting.py tests/unit/test_convergence.py
git commit -m "feat: implement convergence scaling (sector_area, sqrt_sma) in fit_isophote"

docs.title: Task 3: Implement geometry damping (Approach B) in fitting.py
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: isoster/fitting.py:852-871 (geometry update section) - Modify: tests/unit/test_convergence.py (add tests)

Step 1: Write the failing test

Add to tests/unit/test_convergence.py:

class TestGeometryDamping:
    """Test that geometry damping reduces oscillations."""

    def test_damping_reduces_iterations(self):
        """With damping < 1.0, outer isophotes should use fewer iterations."""
        image = make_sersic_image(shape=(401, 401), x0=200, y0=200, re=30.0, eps=0.3)
        mask = np.zeros_like(image, dtype=bool)
        start_geom = {'x0': 200.0, 'y0': 200.0, 'eps': 0.3, 'pa': 0.5}

        cfg_nodamp = IsosterConfig(geometry_damping=1.0, convergence_scaling='none',
                                   maxit=50, minit=5, conver=0.02)
        result_nodamp = fit_isophote(image, mask, 120.0, start_geom, cfg_nodamp)

        cfg_damp = IsosterConfig(geometry_damping=0.7, convergence_scaling='none',
                                 maxit=50, minit=5, conver=0.02)
        result_damp = fit_isophote(image, mask, 120.0, start_geom, cfg_damp)

        # Damped should converge or use fewer iterations
        assert result_damp['niter'] <= result_nodamp['niter'] or result_damp['stop_code'] == 0

    def test_damping_1_is_legacy(self):
        """geometry_damping=1.0 should match legacy behavior."""
        image = make_sersic_image()
        mask = np.zeros_like(image, dtype=bool)
        start_geom = {'x0': 100.0, 'y0': 100.0, 'eps': 0.3, 'pa': 0.5}

        cfg = IsosterConfig(geometry_damping=1.0, convergence_scaling='none')
        result = fit_isophote(image, mask, 30.0, start_geom, cfg)
        assert result['stop_code'] in {0, 1, 2, 3, -1}

    def test_damping_preserves_accuracy(self):
        """Damped fitting should still produce accurate photometry."""
        image = make_sersic_image(shape=(301, 301), x0=150, y0=150, re=30.0, eps=0.3)
        mask = np.zeros_like(image, dtype=bool)
        start_geom = {'x0': 150.0, 'y0': 150.0, 'eps': 0.3, 'pa': 0.5}

        cfg = IsosterConfig(geometry_damping=0.5, convergence_scaling='none')
        result = fit_isophote(image, mask, 30.0, start_geom, cfg)

        # At re=30, intensity should be close to ie (1000) * exp(-bn*(1-1)) = 1000
        assert result['stop_code'] == 0
        assert abs(result['intens'] - 1000.0) / 1000.0 < 0.05  # Within 5%

Step 2: Run test to verify it fails

Run: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_convergence.py::TestGeometryDamping -v

Step 3: Implement geometry damping

In fitting.py, in the geometry update block (lines 852-871), apply damping to the correction before updating:

# Apply geometry damping
damping = cfg.geometry_damping

# Update geometry
if max_idx == 0:
    aux = -max_amp * (1.0 - eps) / gradient * damping
    x0 -= aux * np.sin(pa)
    y0 += aux * np.cos(pa)
elif max_idx == 1:
    aux = -max_amp / gradient * damping
    x0 += aux * np.cos(pa)
    y0 += aux * np.sin(pa)
elif max_idx == 2:
    denom = (1.0 - eps)**2 - 1.0
    if abs(denom) < 1e-10: denom = -1e-10
    pa = (pa + damping * (max_amp * 2.0 * (1.0 - eps) / sma / gradient / denom)) % np.pi
elif max_idx == 3:
    eps = min(eps - damping * (max_amp * 2.0 * (1.0 - eps) / sma / gradient), 0.95)
    if eps < 0.0:
        eps = min(-eps, 0.95)
        pa = (pa + np.pi/2) % np.pi
    if eps == 0.0: eps = 0.05

Step 4: Run tests

Run: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_convergence.py -v Expected: PASS

Step 5: Run full suite

Run: uv run pytest tests/unit/ tests/integration/ tests/validation/ -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tail -20 Expected: 152+ tests pass

Step 6: Commit

git add isoster/fitting.py tests/unit/test_convergence.py
git commit -m "feat: implement geometry damping in fit_isophote"

docs.text:
Files: - Modify: isoster/fitting.py (add geometry tracking and secondary convergence check) - Modify: tests/unit/test_convergence.py (add tests)

Step 1: Write the failing test

Add to tests/unit/test_convergence.py:

class TestGeometryConvergence:
    """Test geometry-stability-based convergence."""

    def test_geometry_convergence_detects_stability(self):
        """When geometry stops changing, should declare convergence."""
        image = make_sersic_image(shape=(401, 401), x0=200, y0=200, re=30.0, eps=0.3)
        mask = np.zeros_like(image, dtype=bool)
        start_geom = {'x0': 200.0, 'y0': 200.0, 'eps': 0.3, 'pa': 0.5}

        # Without geometry convergence: likely hits maxit at outer SMA
        cfg_off = IsosterConfig(geometry_convergence=False, convergence_scaling='none',
                                maxit=30, minit=5, conver=0.01)
        result_off = fit_isophote(image, mask, 150.0, start_geom, cfg_off)

        # With geometry convergence: should converge via geometry stability
        cfg_on = IsosterConfig(geometry_convergence=True, convergence_scaling='none',
                               maxit=30, minit=5, conver=0.01,
                               geometry_tolerance=0.01, geometry_stable_iters=3)
        result_on = fit_isophote(image, mask, 150.0, start_geom, cfg_on)

        # Geometry convergence should help
        assert result_on['niter'] <= result_off['niter'] or result_on['stop_code'] == 0

    def test_geometry_convergence_off_is_legacy(self):
        """geometry_convergence=False should match legacy behavior."""
        image = make_sersic_image()
        mask = np.zeros_like(image, dtype=bool)
        start_geom = {'x0': 100.0, 'y0': 100.0, 'eps': 0.3, 'pa': 0.5}

        cfg = IsosterConfig(geometry_convergence=False, convergence_scaling='none')
        result = fit_isophote(image, mask, 30.0, start_geom, cfg)
        assert result['stop_code'] in {0, 1, 2, 3, -1}

Step 2: Run test to verify it fails

Run: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_convergence.py::TestGeometryConvergence -v

Step 3: Implement geometry-change convergence

In fitting.py, add tracking variables before the loop (after line 684):

# Geometry convergence tracking
prev_geom_x0, prev_geom_y0, prev_geom_eps, prev_geom_pa = x0, y0, eps, pa
stable_count = 0
geom_converged = False

After the geometry update block (after the eps/pa clamping), add:

# Track geometry changes for geometry-based convergence
if cfg.geometry_convergence and i >= minit:
    delta_x0 = abs(x0 - prev_geom_x0) / max(sma, 1.0)
    delta_y0 = abs(y0 - prev_geom_y0) / max(sma, 1.0)
    delta_eps = abs(eps - prev_geom_eps)
    # PA wrapping: use smallest angular difference
    delta_pa_raw = abs(pa - prev_geom_pa)
    delta_pa = min(delta_pa_raw, np.pi - delta_pa_raw) / np.pi
    max_delta = max(delta_x0, delta_y0, delta_eps, delta_pa)

    if max_delta < cfg.geometry_tolerance:
        stable_count += 1
    else:
        stable_count = 0

    if stable_count >= cfg.geometry_stable_iters:
        stop_code = 0  # Converged via geometry stability
        converged = True
        geom_converged = True
        # Compute deviations same as harmonic convergence path
        ...  # (same deviation computation as the harmonic convergence block)
        break

prev_geom_x0, prev_geom_y0, prev_geom_eps, prev_geom_pa = x0, y0, eps, pa

Step 4: Run tests

Run: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_convergence.py -v Expected: PASS

Step 5: Run full suite

Run: uv run pytest tests/unit/ tests/integration/ tests/validation/ -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tail -20 Expected: 152+ tests pass

docs.title: Task 5: Write NGC1209_mock2 diagnostic benchmark script
docs.text:
Files: - Create: benchmarks/convergence_diagnostic.py

Step 1: Write the diagnostic script

This script runs NGC1209_mock2 through five configurations and compares results:

  1. Baseline (convergence_scaling='none', geometry_damping=1.0, geometry_convergence=False)
  2. Approach A (convergence_scaling='sector_area')
  3. Approach A-alt (convergence_scaling='sqrt_sma')
  4. Approach B (geometry_damping=0.7)
  5. Approach C (geometry_convergence=True)
  6. Combined A+B (convergence_scaling='sector_area', geometry_damping=0.7)
  7. Combined A+C (convergence_scaling='sector_area', geometry_convergence=True)

For each, report: - Stop code distribution (count of 0, 1, 2, 3, -1) - Intensity accuracy vs photutils (median/max relative difference at 0.5-8 Re) - Geometry accuracy vs photutils (median/max eps, pa difference at 0.5-8 Re) - Wall-clock time

Output: markdown table to stdout + comparison QA figure to outputs/

The script should: - Load NGC1209_mock2 image from /Users/mac/work/hsc/huang2013/NGC1209/NGC1209_mock2.fits - Load photutils baseline from /Users/mac/work/hsc/huang2013/NGC1209/mock2/NGC1209_mock2_photutils_baseline_profile.fits - Load isoster baseline config from /Users/mac/work/hsc/huang2013/NGC1209/mock2/NGC1209_mock2_isoster_baseline_run.json - Run isoster with each config variant - Compare results

docs.title: Decision
docs.text:
• Default changed: convergence_scaling default set to 'sector_area'
  • Rationale: Eliminates all stop=2, 3.4x faster, best worst-case accuracy
  • B and C: Kept as optional supplementary controls (defaults unchanged)
  • sector_area and sqrt_sma produce identical results on this dataset; sector_area is preferred as it has stronger physical motivation (matches photutils)
