docs.title: Isoster Milestone Code Review -- 2026-02-22
docs.text: Comprehensive review of the isoster codebase at milestone: Huang2013 benchmark campaign completed, basic development reaching maturity. Review covers core library, test suite, examples, documentation, and packaging.

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text:
The isoster codebase is well-architected with clean module separation, correct mathematical foundations (Jedrzejewski 1987 formalism, Ciambur 2015 EA mode), and a solid test harness. The refactoring from monolithic optimize.py into sampling.py, fitting.py, driver.py, and model.py is well done. Pydantic config, numba fallback patterns, and post-run validation are all good engineering decisions.

That said, the review uncovered 1 critical bug (EA + sigma clipping), several important correctness issues, significant test coverage gaps, and documentation staleness that should be addressed before the next development phase.

Severity Count Summary Critical 1 EA mode sigma clipping breaks phi/intens correspondence Important 16 Correctness, robustness, test quality, docs staleness Minor/Suggestion 20+ Code quality, test organization, docs completeness

docs.title: C1. EA mode + sigma clipping causes angle/intensity mismatch -- FIXED
docs.text:
Fixed in: commit 194ad4c (branch fix/ea-sigma-clip-phi-mismatch, merged to main) Regression test: tests/unit/test_fitting.py::test_sigma_clip_ea_phi_sync

File: isoster/fitting.py:666-667

In fit_isophote(), sigma clipping is applied to angles and intens:

angles, intens, n_clipped = sigma_clip(angles, intens, sclip, nclip, sclip_low, sclip_high)

But phi (used for geometry updates and error computation) is not clipped in parallel. After clipping, phi retains its original length while angles and intens are shorter.

  • In regular mode (non-EA), angles == phi, so both are effectively clipped together.
  • In EA mode, angles is psi (eccentric anomaly) and phi is a different array. After clipping, phi has N elements while intens has M < N elements.

This causes mismatched arrays passed to compute_gradient(current_data=(phi, intens)) (line 712) and compute_parameter_errors(phi, intens, ...) (line 756).

Impact: In EA mode with sigma clipping enabled (nclip > 0), gradient and error computations receive mismatched arrays, leading to silent numerical errors or incorrect results.

Fix applied: sigma_clip now accepts extra_arrays parameter; EA mode passes phi through it so the same mask is applied to all arrays.

docs.title: I1. fflag check uses inverted semantics from photutils convention -- FIXED
docs.text:
Fixed in: branch fix/fflag-and-var-residual-guard Regression tests: tests/unit/test_fitting.py::TestFflagSemantics (3 tests)

File: isoster/fitting.py:669

if actual_points < (total_points * fflag):

The fflag parameter is documented as "Maximum fraction of flagged data." With photutils convention, fflag=0.2 means "reject if >20% flagged", so the check should be actual_points < total_points * (1.0 - fflag).

Current behavior: fflag=0.2 allows up to 80% flagging (inverted from user expectation). A strict fflag=0.2 setting is actually very permissive.

Fix applied: Changed to actual_points < total_points * (1.0 - fflag). Note: isoster's fflag check only applies to sigma-clipped points (masking is handled earlier in sampling), unlike photutils where total_points includes mask-rejected points.

docs.title: I2. var_residual can be NaN/Inf when data points <= parameters -- FIXED
docs.text:
Fixed in: branch fix/fflag-and-var-residual-guard Regression tests: tests/unit/test_fitting.py::TestVarResidualDdofGuard (5 tests)

File: isoster/fitting.py:236, 296, 381

var_residual = np.std(intens - model, ddof=len(coeffs))**2

When len(intens) <= len(coeffs), np.std(..., ddof=N) has a zero or negative denominator, producing nan or inf. This propagates silently through error computations.

Same issue in compute_deviations (line 296) and fit_higher_harmonics_simultaneous (line 381).

Fix applied: Added early-return guards (len(intens) <= n_params → return zeros) in compute_parameter_errors (top-level + both code paths), compute_deviations, and fit_higher_harmonics_simultaneous.

docs.title: I3. Gradient formula incorrect in linear growth mode -- FIXED
docs.text:
Fixed in: branch fix/gradient-linear-and-pa-unwrap Regression tests: tests/unit/test_fitting.py::TestGradientLinearGrowth (2 tests)

File: isoster/fitting.py:480

gradient = (mean_g - mean_c) / sma / step

For geometric growth: delta_r = sma * step, so gradient = (mean_g - mean_c) / (sma * step) -- correct.

For linear growth: delta_r = step, so gradient should be (mean_g - mean_c) / step, but the code divides by sma * step -- incorrect by a factor of sma.

Fix: 

delta_r = sma * step if not linear_growth else step
gradient = (mean_g - mean_c) / delta_r

docs.title: I4. model.py does not filter NaN intensities before interpolation -- FIXED
docs.text:
Fixed in: branch fix/gradient-linear-and-pa-unwrap Regression tests: tests/unit/test_model.py::TestModel::test_nan_isophotes_filtered_before_interpolation, test_nan_geometry_filtered_before_interpolation, test_nan_harmonic_coefficients_treated_as_zero

File: isoster/model.py:103-104

Isophotes with stop_code != 0 may have intens = np.nan. These are included in the interpolation arrays, causing interp1d to produce NaN across the model.

Fix applied: Filter isophotes requiring finite values in all 5 key columns (intens, x0, y0, eps, pa). Additionally, non-finite harmonic coefficients are replaced with 0.0 to prevent poisoning the harmonic interpolation.

docs.title: I5. PA interpolation in model.py wraps incorrectly at 0/180 boundary -- FIXED
docs.text:
Fixed in: branch fix/gradient-linear-and-pa-unwrap Regression test: tests/unit/test_model.py::TestModel::test_pa_interpolation_wrap_boundary

File: isoster/model.py:140

Linear interpolation of position angle values that wrap (e.g., 170 to 10 degrees) produces incorrect intermediate values (~90 degrees) instead of interpolating through the boundary.

Fix: Apply np.unwrap() to PA values before interpolation.

docs.title: I6. add_cog_to_isophotes has no length check against CoG results -- FIXED
docs.text:
Fixed in: branch fix/gradient-linear-and-pa-unwrap Regression test: tests/integration/test_edge_cases.py::TestAddCogLengthCheck::test_length_mismatch_raises

File: isoster/cog.py:188-193

If the input list lengths don't match, cog_results['cog'][i] raises an IndexError.

Fix applied: Added explicit length check raising ValueError with a clear message before the loop.

docs.title: I7. Missing valid field in forced photometry results -- FIXED
docs.text:
Fixed in: branch fix/gradient-linear-and-pa-unwrap Regression test: tests/integration/test_edge_cases.py::TestForcedMode::test_forced_photometry_has_valid_field_on_failure

File: isoster/fitting.py:117-126

extract_forced_photometry returns dicts without a valid field, while fit_central_pixel includes it. Consumers checking for this field will get a KeyError.

Fix applied: Added 'valid': True to the success return and 'valid': False to the stop_code=3 early return.

docs.title: I8. Stop code 2 path does not compute harmonic deviations -- FIXED
docs.text:
Fixed in: branch fix/gradient-linear-and-pa-unwrap Regression test: tests/unit/test_fitting.py::test_stop_code_2_computes_harmonic_deviations

File: isoster/fitting.py:842-845

When maxit is reached (stop_code=2), the code attaches photometry but NOT higher-order harmonic deviations. a3, b3, a4, b4 are all 0.0 for these isophotes, even though a best-effort computation is available from the last iteration.

Fix applied: Added best-effort harmonic computation (both sequential and simultaneous paths) in the stop_code=2 block, using the last iteration's angles, intens, and gradient.

docs.title: Isoster Code Review — 2026-02-26
docs.text:
Incremental review covering changes since the 2026-02-22 review (24 items, all fixed). Focus areas: simultaneous geometry updates, isofit_mode config, config validation (V1-V11), IC3370 exhaustive benchmark, and the plot_qa_summary direct residual change.

Baseline: main at a23e5ce, 203 tests passing.

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text:
The new features are well-implemented and well-tested. The config validation suite (V1-V11) is thorough with both positive and negative test cases. The simultaneous geometry update mode is correctly derived from Jedrzejewski (1987) formulas. The IC3370 exhaustive benchmark is a well-designed systematic evaluation of all config parameters.

One important bug was found (R26-01: second-gradient formula wrong for linear growth), plus a design issue with the forced photometry API that needs rethinking (R26-05). Several code quality issues and minor concerns are noted.

Severity Count Summary Critical 0 — Design 1 Forced photometry API needs unification with template mode Important 4 Gradient bug, forced photometry inconsistency, code duplication, V5 noise Minor 8 Comment errors, fragile checks, function size, test weakness Test gaps 4 Missing integration tests for new features

docs.title: Task IDs
docs.text:
All findings use the prefix R26- (Review 2026-02-26) for traceability.

Task ID Severity Summary Priority Status R26-01 Important Second-gradient formula wrong for linear growth P1 Open R26-02 Important extract_forced_photometry hardcodes harmonic keys P2 Open R26-03 Important Post-hoc harmonic code duplicated 3x in fit_isophote P2 Open R26-04 Important V5 warning fires for every forced-mode config P3 Open R26-05 Design Unify forced photometry: replace forced/forced_sma with template-based API P2 Open R26-06 Minor V9 test assertion always true P3 Open R26-07 Minor Wrong comment in extract_forced_photometry P4 Open R26-08 Minor var_residual recomputed per coefficient in ISOFIT loop P4 Open R26-09 Minor fit_isophote ~450 lines, hard to maintain P3 Open R26-10 Minor model.py has_harmonics checks only first isophote P4 Open R26-11 Minor Stop codes 4,5 defined in plotting but never produced P4 Open R26-12 Minor Vestigial duck typing in compute_gradient P4 Open R26-13 Minor No key validation in template forced mode P3 Open R26-14 Minor Default residual type change undocumented P4 Open R26-T1 Test Add integration test: geometry_update_mode='simultaneous' with fit_image P3 Open R26-T2 Test Add integration test: isofit_mode='original' post-hoc harmonics P3 Open R26-T3 Test Add test for invalid convergence_scaling values P4 Open R26-T4 Test Strengthen V9 test assertion (same as R26-06) P3 Open

docs.title: R26-05. Forced photometry API needs unification with template mode
docs.text:
Files: isoster/config.py:102-104, isoster/driver.py:92-163, 276-344

The current API has two overlapping mechanisms for forced photometry:

  1. forced=True + forced_sma=[...] (config-based): Uses a single fixed geometry (x0, y0, eps, pa from config) at specified SMA values. The geometry is the same for all SMA values.

  2. template_isophotes=[...] (function parameter): Uses variable geometry per SMA from a previous isoster run. Each SMA gets its own (x0, y0, eps, pa) from the template.

Problems with the current design:

  • The forced mode with a single fixed geometry is nearly useless in practice — real galaxies have varying geometry with radius. The useful workflow is always template-based.
  • The V6 warning (template_isophotes takes priority over forced=True) acknowledges the overlap but doesn't resolve it.
  • The naming is confusing: both mechanisms do "forced photometry" but through different code paths with different capabilities.
  • Template isophotes can only be passed as an in-memory list. Users must manually call isophote_results_from_fits() to load a template file. The common multiband workflow should accept a file path directly.
  • The forced_sma config field is a vestigial concept — SMA values should come from the template, not be specified separately.

Proposed redesign (for discussion):

Replace forced=True/forced_sma with a single template parameter that accepts: - A file path (string) → loads via isophote_results_from_fits() - A results dict (from a previous fit_image() call) → extracts ['isophotes'] - A list of isophote dicts (current template_isophotes behavior)

The config fields forced and forced_sma would be deprecated. The template_isophotes function parameter would be renamed to template for brevity.

# New API (proposed)
results_r = isoster.fit_image(image_r, mask_r, config,
                               template="galaxy_g.fits")
# or
results_r = isoster.fit_image(image_r, mask_r, config,
                               template=results_g)
# or (current behavior, still supported)
results_r = isoster.fit_image(image_r, mask_r, config,
                               template=results_g['isophotes'])

Scope: This is an API design change that should be discussed before implementation. It affects config.py, driver.py, __init__.py exports, CLAUDE.md, user-facing docs, and possibly cli.py.

docs.title: R26-01. Second-gradient formula still wrong for linear growth mode
docs.text:
File: isoster/fitting.py:642-645

The first gradient was correctly fixed in I3 (line 606-607): 

delta_r = step if linear_growth else sma * step
gradient = (mean_g - mean_c) / delta_r

But the second-gradient fallback path (lines 642-645) was NOT fixed: 

gradient = (mean_g2 - mean_c) / sma / (2 * step)          # line 642
gradient_error = (...) / sma / (2 * step)                  # lines 644-645

For linear growth, gradient_sma_2 = sma + 2 * step, so delta_r = 2 * step. The formula should be / (2 * step), not / sma / (2 * step). Current code is wrong by a factor of sma.

For geometric growth, delta_r ≈ sma * 2 * step, so / sma / (2 * step) is correct.

Fix: 

delta_r_2 = 2 * step if linear_growth else sma * 2 * step
gradient = (mean_g2 - mean_c) / delta_r_2
gradient_error = (...) / delta_r_2

Impact: Only affects linear growth mode (linear_growth=True) when the second-gradient path is triggered. Geometric growth (the default) is unaffected. Low practical impact since linear growth is rarely used, but it's a correctness issue.

docs.title: R26-02. extract_forced_photometry hardcodes harmonic keys regardless of config
docs.text:
File: isoster/fitting.py:100-103, 119-127

extract_forced_photometry always includes a3, b3, a3_err, b3_err, a4, b4, a4_err, b4_err in its return dict — both in the empty-data early return (line 100-103) and the success return (line 119-127). These are always zero since forced photometry doesn't fit harmonics.

This is inconsistent with the V8 cleanup where fit_isophote now only includes harmonic keys when compute_deviations or simultaneous_harmonics is enabled. The V8 test (test_no_harmonic_keys_when_disabled) validates that fit_isophote doesn't produce these keys, but forced photometry still does.

Impact: Not a correctness bug — the zeros are benign. But it breaks the V8 contract that harmonic keys are absent when deviations are disabled. This inconsistency could confuse downstream code that uses key presence to detect whether harmonics were fitted.

Note: This issue is subsumed by R26-05 — a forced photometry API redesign would address the output schema as part of the unification.

docs.title: R26-03. Post-hoc harmonic deviation code duplicated three times
docs.text:
File: isoster/fitting.py:998-1025, 1098-1119, 1142-1164

The identical 20-line block for computing post-hoc harmonic deviations appears at: 1. Harmonic convergence (line 998-1025) 2. Geometry convergence (line 1098-1119) 3. Stop code 2 fallback (line 1142-1164)

All three blocks contain: - The use_best_data flag logic - The posthoc_angles/posthoc_intens/posthoc_gradient selection - The simultaneous vs sequential branching - The per-order result storage loop

This violates DRY and is a maintenance hazard — any future change to the harmonic computation logic must be applied to all three locations, or they will diverge.

Fix: Extract a helper function: 

def _compute_posthoc_harmonics(best_geometry, angles, intens, gradient,
                                best_angles, best_intens, best_gradient,
                                sma, harmonic_orders, isofit_mode,
                                simultaneous_harmonics):
    ...

docs.title: R26-04. V5 warning fires for every forced-mode config due to defaults
docs.text:
File: isoster/config.py:249-266

The V5 validation checks whether compute_errors=True and compute_deviations=True when forced=True, and warns that these are "ignored". But both are defaults — compute_errors defaults to True and compute_deviations defaults to True.

This means IsosterConfig(forced=True, forced_sma=[5.0, 10.0]) ALWAYS produces a warning: 

UserWarning: forced=True ignores: compute_errors, compute_deviations

The test test_no_warn_forced_defaults (line 78-81) confirms this is intentional by expecting the warning. However, it's noisy for users.

Note: This issue is subsumed by R26-05 — a forced photometry API redesign would eliminate the forced config flag entirely.

docs.title: Part 3: Minor Issues
docs.text: Task ID File Issue R26-07 fitting.py:92 Comment says "φ (EA not applicable at central pixel)" but extract_forced_photometry handles arbitrary SMA, not just central pixel. R26-08 fitting.py:984-988 In ISOFIT in_loop best-geometry block, var_residual is recomputed per harmonic coefficient pair inside the for-loop. Should compute once before the loop. R26-09 fitting.py:723-1169 fit_isophote is ~450 lines. With the three duplicated harmonic blocks (R26-03), this is increasingly hard to maintain. Consider extracting convergence/photometry/harmonics into helpers. R26-10 model.py:218-220 has_harmonics check only inspects sorted_isos[0]. If the first valid isophote lacks harmonic keys but others have them, harmonics are skipped. Unlikely in practice (keys are consistently initialized) but fragile. R26-11 plotting.py:29-31 STOP_CODE_STYLES defines stop codes 4 and 5 which isoster never produces. Likely photutils-compatibility placeholders; no harm but could confuse readers. R26-12 fitting.py:568-571 compute_gradient uses if hasattr(config, 'astep') duck typing, but the caller always passes a plain dict. The duck typing is vestigial. R26-13 driver.py:318 _fit_image_template_forced doesn't validate that template dicts contain required keys ('x0', 'y0', 'eps', 'pa'). Missing keys produce an unhelpful KeyError. R26-14 plotting.py:440 plot_qa_summary default changed from fractional to direct residual. Deliberate and documented, but should be noted in a changelog/release notes.

docs.title: Part 4: Config Validation Review (V1-V11)
docs.text: The config validation implementation in config.py and test suite in test_config_validation.py are well done. Specific observations:

docs.title: Positive
docs.text:
• Clean separation of hard errors (ValueError) vs soft warnings (UserWarning)
  • Every validation has both positive and negative test cases
  • Warning messages are specific and actionable (e.g., V4 suggests damping=0.5)
  • V7 (central_reg_weights key validation) correctly uses set comparison
  • V10 formula maxit < minit + geometry_stable_iters is mathematically correct
  • V6 is correctly placed in driver.py (runtime check) since template_isophotes is a function parameter, not a config field

docs.title: Concerns
docs.text: Task ID Issue Severity R26-06 V9 test assertion assert 'ndata' in central or 'valid' in central is always true — valid is present even without debug=True. Should assert for 'ndata' alone. Minor R26-04 V5 fires on defaults (see above) Important R26-T3 No test for invalid convergence_scaling values — the regex pattern in the Field definition handles this, but an explicit test would document the behavior Minor

docs.title: Part 5: Simultaneous Geometry Updates Review
docs.text:
The implementation in fitting.py:1034-1055 is mathematically correct:

  1. Center corrections (harmonics[0], harmonics[1]): Same Jedrzejewski (1987) formulas as the largest mode, applied independently for minor and major axis corrections.
  2. PA correction (harmonics[2]): Same formula with proper denominator guard.
  3. Ellipticity correction (harmonics[3]): Same formula with eps bounds and PA flip.
  4. Damping: Applied consistently to all four corrections.
  5. Fix flags: if not fix_center/fix_pa/fix_eps guards correctly skip fixed parameters.
  6. Zeroed harmonics: Fixed harmonics are zeroed at lines 924-926 before max_idx selection, so min_amplitude tracking still works correctly.

docs.title: Test Coverage
docs.text:
The 6 new tests provide good coverage: - Config validation (accepts valid, rejects invalid) - Convergence on circular and elliptical mocks - Equivalence with largest mode on well-behaved data - Iteration count comparison (simultaneous ≤ largest + tolerance) - Fixed-geometry respected

Gap (R26-T1): No integration test runs fit_image end-to-end with geometry_update_mode='simultaneous'. The existing tests only exercise fit_isophote (single SMA).

docs.title: Part 6: ISOFIT Mode Review
docs.text:
The isofit_mode implementation is clean: - 'in_loop': Full extended design matrix inside iteration loop (lines 874-876). Coefficients correctly extracted from joint fit. - 'original': 5-param fit inside loop, post-hoc simultaneous fitting after convergence (lines 1000-1017). Uses saved best-iteration data.

Both paths share the same harmonic normalization by sma * |gradient|. No issues found.

Gap (R26-T2): No integration test for isofit_mode='original' post-hoc harmonics.

docs.title: Part 7: IC3370 Exhaustive Benchmark Review
docs.text:
benchmarks/ic3370_exhausted/config_registry.py is well-structured: - Clear separation of baseline overrides, photutils config, and per-config deltas - Sensible parameter sweep choices covering all major config dimensions - Combination configs (C01-C12) test important feature interactions - EXTENDED_HARMONIC_CONFIGS and NEEDS_REFERENCE_GEOMETRY sets for runtime logic

No issues found.

docs.title: Part 8: plot_qa_summary Direct Residual Change
docs.text:
The change from fractional to direct residual as default is well-implemented: - relative_residual=False (new default): computes image - isoster_model - relative_residual=True (old behavior): computes 100 * (model - data) / data - Color scale limits derived from data (99th percentile for direct, clipped [0.05, 8.0] for fractional) - Colorbar label correctly updates between modes

Note: the sign convention differs between modes — direct residual uses data - model while fractional uses model - data. This should be documented.
